subject: Sociology
title: Strain Theory
description: A quick, practical guide to Strain Theory.

hook.text: Society tells everyone to get rich. Then it blocks most people's path. Robert Merton saw crime in that gap.

buildup.text: Merton's strain theory says crime results from the gap between cultural goals and legitimate means to reach them.

discovery.text: When legal paths are blocked, people innovate: they steal, hustle, or cheat to achieve what society demands.

twist.text: Not everyone responds with crime. Some retreat, rebel, or simply conform despite the odds against them.

climax.text: Strain theory explains why crime concentrates among the poor — not because of values, but blocked opportunity.

punchline.text: Block the path and people find detours.

quiz.question: What does Merton's strain theory argue?
options: Crime results from the gap between goals and legitimate means
